title: DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning in Open Language Models

summary.problem: Mathematical reasoning poses a significant challenge for language models. Open-source models lag far behind closed-source systems like GPT-4 on competition-level math benchmarks.
summary.method: Continue pre-training DeepSeek-Coder-Base 7B on 500B tokens (including 120B math tokens from Common Crawl), then SFT on 776K examples, and finally reinforce with GRPO. GRPO eliminates the critic model by sampling G outputs per question and computing advantages from group-relative normalized rewards.
summary.innovation: GRPO replaces PPO's per-token value function with a group-relative baseline estimated from multiple sampled outputs per question, cutting memory footprint roughly in half. The paper also provides a unified gradient-based framework showing RFT, DPO, PPO, and GRPO as special cases.
summary.results: DeepSeekMath 7B achieves 51.7% on MATH and 88.2% on GSM8K without external tools, surpassing all open-source models up to 70B. GRPO adds +4.9% on MATH and +5.3% on GSM8K over SFT.
summary.one_liner: DeepSeekMath 用 120B 高质量数学语料 + GRPO（无 critic 的 PPO 变体，用组内相对奖励做 baseline）把 7B 模型在 MATH 上刷到 51.7%。

key_references.title: Minerva: Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models
